name: Pork Chops with Grapes
recipeIngredient: 4 pork loin or rib chops, about 1/2 inch thick (about 1 pound)
1 teaspoon chopped fresh tarragon leaves or 1/4 teaspoon dried tarragon leaves
1 can mushroom stems and pieces, drained (4-ounce)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from pork chops.
2. Cook pork in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat, turning once, until brown; drain.
3. Sprinkle with tarragon, salt, and pepper.
4. Mix broth, lemon juice, and garlic; pour over pork.
5. Heat to boiling; reduce heat.
6. Cover and simmer about 30 minutes or until pork is tender. Remove pork; keep warm.
7. Skim any fat from liquid in skillet.
8. Mix cornstarch and cold water; gradually stir into liquid.
9. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly. Boil and stir 1 minute.
10. Stir in grapes and mushrooms; heat just until hot.
11. Serve sauce over pork. 4 servings
